items.headline: City Council approves Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: The City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo to be installed at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument will be a permanent public art addition to the park.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council is updating the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan, which guides development and policy through 2040. This update addresses how the city plans to manage public health, safety, and emergency preparedness over the next decade.

items.headline: City Council Appoints New Permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent city manager. This is a significant leadership decision for the city's executive administration.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates water shortage plan amid drought concerns
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission is reviewing the Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The plan outlines how the city will manage water supply during shortages, a critical issue for South Bay residents facing ongoing drought conditions.

items.headline: Spring community grants announced for neighborhoods and events
items.summary: City Council is considering applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program's spring cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can learn which community projects and local events have been selected for funding.

items.headline: City to develop principles for residential electrification programs
items.summary: Palo Alto is developing proposed principles to guide residential electrification programs designed to help comply with air quality regulations. The update addresses the city's transition away from gas-powered heating and appliances to electric alternatives.

items.headline: City Council to amend zoning rules for accessory dwelling units
items.summary: Palo Alto is considering changes to municipal code chapters governing accessory dwelling units (ADUs) and junior accessory dwelling units (JADUs) to align with recent state law changes and prior City Council direction. This affects housing options for residents looking to add secondary units on their properties.

items.headline: Stream Corridor Protection ordinance amendment proposed
items.summary: The City Council is reviewing an amendment to the stream corridor protection provisions in the municipal code. This could affect development rules near waterways and environmental protections in Palo Alto.
